title: Prism3 BrandInput
description: The white-label brand-input contract: the small, validated input set the engine expands into a complete token layer. This schema is the canonical, faithful description of the engine's `BrandInput` type (engine/theme.ts) — it is validated against on every emit. The New Balance REGRESSION uses a different, richer measurement fixture (schema/nb-measured.json), not this contract.

id.description: Brand identifier (e.g. 'aurora'). Host-supplied identity; the emit namespace is set separately by `root`.

root.description: The single, mode-invariant token namespace segment. Every token emits under `<root>.*` (colour primitives at `<root>.palette`, semantic roles at `<root>.color`). Per-engagement/brand; defaults to the placeholder 'prism'. One segment only — no dots (two-segment namespaces are intentionally unsupported).

modes.description: OPTIONAL. Appearance modes to generate. `light` is the required base; `dark` / `hc-light` / `hc-dark` are opt-in. Omit for all four (back-compat); a light-only brand sets `["light"]`. Must include `light`.

primary.description: The exact brand anchor (palette 'primary'). Pinned, never shifted; the engine auto-places it on the step ramp by its lightness.

hue.description: Neutral ramp hue (deg). A small chroma tints the greys toward the brand for cohesion.

chroma.description: Peak neutral chroma (~0.004–0.02); tapers to near-0 at the ramp ends.

anchor.description: OPTIONAL. A pre-defined brand grey, pinned verbatim at its lightness step; the whole neutral ramp is built around it (hue/chroma from the anchor) instead of the hue/chroma cast. For a client that ships their own neutral.

neutral.description: The neutral ramp generator: hue + peak chroma (derived), or an exact `anchor` grey (pinned).

brandColors.description: OPTIONAL. Additional brand colours (secondary / tertiary / accents) — any number. Each becomes its own ramp and can be named by `actionPalette`. This is what makes the palette set open-ended.

actionPalette.description: OPTIONAL. Which palette drives the interactive/action colour. Defaults to 'primary'. Brands whose hero colour is unsuitable for actions name another palette here (an accent, or even neutral). The engine FLAGS this in notes so it is an explicit, confirmable choice.

surfaces.description: OPTIONAL. Non-default primary surfaces per mode (e.g. a warm off-white page). The contrast FLOOR moves with the declared base; the engine flags it in notes. Omit for white/black defaults.

status.description: OPTIONAL. Measured status hue overrides; omit any to let the engine synthesise from canonical hues (and carve a dedicated danger red when the primary isn't red).

disabledStrategy.description: OPTIONAL. How disabled states are coloured. 'accessible' (default): disabled text/icon/border clears `disabledMin` on the contrast floor, staying legible (the practice's contrast-preserving stance; field-rare — 0/12 surveyed systems guarantee it). 'conventional': intentionally sub-AA, leaning on the WCAG 1.4.3/1.4.11 inactive-component exemption (the field-standard dimmed look).

disabledMin.description: OPTIONAL. Contrast floor for the 'accessible' disabled strategy (default 3:1 — visibly dimmed but legible, where Primer/USWDS sit; escalates to 4.5:1 in high-contrast modes). Ignored when disabledStrategy is 'conventional'.

iconContrast.description: OPTIONAL. Icon contrast floor. 'text' (default): icons mirror text at 4.5:1. '3:1': icons resolve against the WCAG SC 1.4.11 non-text floor (graphical objects), letting secondary/semantic icons run lighter than text; icon.primary stays strong.

motionPersonality.description: OPTIONAL — the disciplined 6th input. Brand motion character. `tempo` scales the generated duration ramp; reduce-motion variants are always derived (informational preserved, vestibular → 0).

tempo.description: Scales the base duration ramp (snappy ×0.8, standard ×1.0, relaxed ×1.3).

easingEmphasized.description: Optional cubic-bezier override for the expressive 'emphasized' easing curve.

typography.description: OPTIONAL. Typography axis lever. The rem size ladder is brand-invariant (curated, not ratio-derived); the brand-variable parts are the families, the weight role→numeric map, and the `typeScale` preset (consumed at the semantic tier).

families.description: Display / text / mono faces. A single face name is auto-padded with a system fallback stack; a full array is trusted as-is. `variable` flags variable-font usage.

display.description: Display/hero face (defaults to the text face if omitted).

variable.description: Whether the faces are variable fonts.

weightRoles.description: Function-named weight roles → the brand's numeric weights (the white-label-stable weight model). Omit any to use the defaults (subtle 300 / default 400 / emphasis 600 / strong 700).

emphasis.description: Mild lift — strong inline text, active states.

typeScale.description: Shifts heading sizes (display + title) up/down the ladder: expressive → one rung higher, compact → one lower. Body/label/caption stay put.

familyMap.description: Which family role each semantic group consumes. Defaults: display/title/label/eyebrow → display (brand); body/caption → text; code → mono. Override per group (e.g. neutral buttons: { label: 'text' }). Family is a property of the group, not the size.

displayCeiling.description: Cap the display tier (px). Brands that don't need mega heroes stop lower (e.g. 96); the ladder is unchanged, the engine just omits display composites above the cap.

titleFloor.description: Smallest title size. Default 18 (title.xs). Set 16 to add title.2xs — a 16px brand-font heading that deliberately overlaps body.md.

responsive.description: Responsive sizing. `fluid` (default true) gives heading groups a mobile endpoint (desktop × a per-group factor, snapped to the ladder); the one min/max pair drives both the web clamp() and the Figma desktop/mobile modes. Reading/UI text stays static.

fluid.description: Fluid heading sizing on/off. Off → all sizes static (min == max).

minViewport.description: Viewport (px) at which fluid type reaches its min.

maxViewport.description: Viewport (px) at which fluid type reaches its max.

weights.description: Per-role weight set — weight is an axis on every type role, and every composite carries the weight in its name (type.<group>.<size>.<weight>). Defaults: display/title ['strong'], body ['default','strong'] (add 'emphasis' for a 3rd), caption ['default','strong'], label/eyebrow ['emphasis'], code ['default']. Override a role to ship a multi-weight ramp (e.g. display: ['default','strong']). Adding a weight is additive — never renames.

links.description: Which roles get an underlined `.<weight>-link` variant for every size×weight (inline links inherit the surrounding size+weight). Default ['body','caption']. Underline is baked (not Figma-bindable); the link colour stays text.link.*.

shadow.description: OPTIONAL. Shadow / elevation axis. A 6-step ramp (xs–2xl) + inset, 2-layer (key+ambient), mode-aware (full in light; reduced in dark — the surface ladder carries dark elevation, the lift-primary model).

softness.description: Blur:offset personality dial. Low → crisp/product; high → soft/marketing. Scales blur across the ramp.

tint.description: Hue-shifts the shadow base off pure black. Default a subtle neutral tint; set a brand hue + higher amount for brand-hued marketing shadows.

hue.description: Shadow tint hue (deg). Defaults to the neutral ramp hue.

amount.description: Tint strength (0 ≈ pure black; ~1 ≈ clearly hue-tinted). Default 0.15.

layout.description: OPTIONAL. Layout axis — breakpoints + responsive grid + containers. 5 breakpoints (min-width/mobile-first), a 12-col design grid (4/8/12 ladder), gutters/margins aliasing the spacing scale, fluid-first containers + a max cap.

breakpoints.description: Min-width floors (px), ascending. Names are auto sm/md/lg/xl/2xl. Default [0,768,1024,1440,1920].

columns.description: Base column count (12 default; 16/24 for dense-data brands). Emitted as a 4/8/…/base ladder per breakpoint — a design grid, not the load-bearing code contract.

containerMax.description: Content max-width cap (px); layout is fluid below it.

gradients.description: OPTIONAL, OPT-IN (off by default — most systems abstain). `true` ships one default brand gradient (primary.600→primary.350, linear); an array ships exactly the listed gradients. Stop colours alias the colour ramp; OKLCH interpolation by default; materializes as a Figma Paint Style (only stop colours bind).

spaceBase.description: OPTIONAL. Spacing rhythm (px) for the numbered-multiplier space scale. Default 8.

radiusScale.description: OPTIONAL. Corner softness: 0 = sharp, 1 = default, 2 = soft. Default 1.

baseMd.description: OPTIONAL. The radius.md anchor (px) at scale 1. Default 4.

gradient.description: A brand gradient. Stops alias the colour ramp by {palette, step}; kind/angle/interpolation are carried because DTCG's gradient type omits them.

kind.description: linear (with angle) or radial (with center + shape).

interpolation.description: Interpolation colour space. OKLCH avoids the sRGB grey dead zone; Figma gets a pre-sampled sRGB approximation.

samples.description: Number of sRGB stops to pre-sample for Figma (which interpolates in sRGB only).

stops.description: Two or more colour stops, aliasing the ramp.

position.description: Stop position 0..1 along the gradient axis.

chroma.description: Peak ramp chroma for this status hue.

base.description: The page surface for this mode.

floorStep.description: The neutral step used as the contrast floor; defaults follow `base`.
